claim_text: The InP substrate market is concentrated, with Sumitomo, AXT/Tongmei, and JX accounting for most share.
AXT directly links InP demand to data-center connectivity, AI applications, PICs, and silicon photonics.
notes: Direct support for AI relevance of InP.

claim_text: China added InP substrates to its export-control list on February 4, 2025.
notes: Also reiterated in AXT 2025 Q1 filing.

claim_text: AXT manufactures all wafer substrates in China.
NVIDIA is moving toward silicon photonics and co-packaged optics in AI networking.
Broadcom is pushing third-generation co-packaged optics further into AI networking.
Lumentum ties InP lasers to AI-driven co-packaged optics demand.
Coherent is expanding 6-inch InP-related capacity outside the narrow substrate-duopoly framing.
notes: Capacity expansion weakens the strongest scarcity framing.

claim_text: JX Advanced Metals is adding InP substrate capacity in response to AI and hyperscale optical demand.
notes: Shows live capacity response from a smaller supplier.

claim_text: Google TPU v7 created unprecedented strain on the InP supply chain.
notes: Public TPU docs support photonics direction but do not prove the exact claim.

claim_text: The AI industry can be frozen by two InP suppliers alone.
notes: Too absolute based on currently verified evidence.
